claim: The text-only section (entropy 3.9016 bits, 7 folios) represents the closest approximation to natural language and encodes a different register or genre than the herbal/zodiac sections, such that its bigram entropy is statistically distinguishable (p < 0.05) from all other sections and its type-token ratio is highest among all sections
evidence_for: Text-only entropy (3.9016) is highest of all sections and closest to Latin (~4.0) and Italian (~3.95)
Absence of illustrations may mean less label-like, more prose-like text
Recipes section (3.8586) and text-only are the two highest-entropy sections, both likely prose
evidence_against: Only 7 folios â€” small sample may inflate entropy estimate
High hapax ratio across the whole manuscript makes type-token ratio comparisons noisy
test: Compute per-section bigram conditional entropy H(glyph_n | glyph_{n-1}) and type-token ratio (unique words / total words); run Kruskal-Wallis test across sections, then post-hoc pairwise Mann-Whitney U between text-only and each other section
test_metric: Bigram conditional entropy and type-token ratio per section
test_threshold: Text-only bigram entropy significantly higher (p < 0.05) than herbal and zodiac sections; text-only type-token ratio > 0.35 vs herbal < 0.25 would confirm distinct register

tests_run.test: compute per-section bigram conditional entropy h(glyph_n | glyph_{n-1}) and type-token ratio (unique
tests_run.details: Observed glyph entropy: 3.8627 bits. Expected for hypothesis: ~4.0 bits. Difference: 0.1373 bits.

reasoning: If text-only is linguistically distinct from illustrated sections, it should be the primary target for language identification; confirming this concentrates decipherment resources on the highest-signal section
